---
title: "Mitigation Coverage"
---

This chart shows which what defensive controls would reduce the effectiveness of the techniques Volt Typhoon is known to use. For example, ATT&CK identifies Privileged Account Management as a mitigation for 39 documented Volt Typhoon technique relationships.

<iframe src="figures/volt_typhoon_mitigation_coverage.html" width="100%" height="650"></iframe>  

## So What?  

 - The numbers represent breadth of defensive coverage, not strength of protection. Higher values indicate controls that are relevant to a larger share of Volt Typhoon's documented tradecraft. <br>
 - The mitigations most frequently associated with Volt Typhoon techniques focus on account security, auditing, and privilege management.<br>
 - This suggests that the most effective defense against Volt Typhoon is not traditional malware detection, but rather strong identity security, continuous monitoring, and limiting adversaries' ability to abuse legitimate credentials.
